etymology.description: semantic 力 (strength) + phonetic 工 (work) -> work plus strength -> achieving results
etymology.interpretation: The character 功 (gōng, "achievement; skill") combines 力(力) (lì, "strength") as the semantic component, indicating the effort or energy expended, with 工(工) (gōng, "work") as the phonetic, also emphasizing the idea of labor. The combination implies utilizing strength in work to achieve success or skill, highlighting the relationship between effort and achievement.

examples.chinese: 他花了很多时间在功课上(他花了很多時間在功課上)。
examples.pinyin: Tā huāle hěnduō shíjiān zài gōngkè shàng.
examples.english: He spent a lot of time on his homework.

examples.chinese: 努力工作会带来成功(努力工作會帶來成功)。
这个计划终于取得了预期的成功(這個計劃終於取得了預期的成功)。
examples.pinyin: Zhège jìhuà zhōngyú qǔdéle yùqī de chénggōng.
examples.english: The plan finally achieved the expected success.
